concerns.text: Task T012 explicitly instructs the model to 'Explicitly ignore the MI diagnostic report from T016 for feature dropping.' This violates FR-007 which mandates the system to 'frame their joint relationship as descriptive rather than claiming independent predictive effects.' Ignoring the diagnostic prevents the required framing of the limitation in the model's behavior/output, effectively weakening the requirement to report and contextualize the dependency.

Task T016 defines 'Case B' where the MI diagnostic is skipped if no valid Σ values exist, producing only a text report stating 'unavailable'. FR-007 requires the system to 'report mutual information (MI) scores'. If the data is imperfect (no valid Σ), the task fails to produce the required score/report, violating the mandatory nature of the functional requirement.

Task T017 performs k=5 CV on the 'training set' to assess stability, while SC-001 requires R² standard deviation to be reported 'across k=5 cross-validation folds' measured against the 'held-out test set' (per US-1 acceptance scenario). The task decouples the stability metric from the test-set evaluation required by the spec, creating a semantic drift where the reported SD does not reflect the test-set performance variance mandated by SC-001.

Task T010 instructs to 'Output the Rodrigues vector components (x, y, z) as distinct features.' FR-002 requires encoding misorientation using 'Rodrigues vectors'. Flattening the vector into three distinct scalar features without preserving the vectorial relationship or symmetry constraints in the feature engineering step risks violating the mathematical integrity of the encoding required for the model to correctly interpret rotational symmetry as a single geometric entity.

Task T021 hardcodes the sensitivity sweep to exactly three discrete points {0.70, 0.75, 0.80}. SC-004 requires sweeping R² threshold across '{moderate, high, very high}' (implied as a range or dynamic benchmarks). Hardcoding specific values without allowing for dynamic definition based on the 'community-standard' justification (referenced in SC-004) creates a rigid implementation that may not satisfy the 'robustness' requirement if the benchmarks shift or if the spec implies a continuous or broader range.
